title: guibourtia gossweileri
intro: Guibourtia gossweileri, also known as Mupani, is a tree species native to southern Africa, particularly in Angola and Namibia. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Guibourtia gossweileri include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as acting as a natural insect repellent. Traditionally, the bark and roots of the tree are used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are made by boiling the bark or roots in water to release their medicinal properties.
